title: Using and adapting the mizer extension template
text: mizerExtensionTemplate working mizer extension package can clone adapt. Every line code commented explain done . Read source files alongside vignette. full conceptual background see: vignette("extending-mizer", package = "mizer") — five extension mechanisms worked examples. vignette("creating-extension-packages", package = "mizer") — turning script composable, shareable package.

title: Using and adapting the mizer extension template
text: mizerExtensionTemplate adds three things standard mizer model:

title: Using and adapting the mizer extension template
text: package ships ready-made example_params object — three-species (Sprat, Herring, Cod) model built newExtensionTemplateParams(). stored data/example_params.rda lazy-loaded R, .onLoad hook replaces plain binding active binding every access returns object correct S4 extension class: can use directly without calling newExtensionTemplateParams():
code: class(example_params)   # mizerExtensionTemplate, not plain MizerParams #> [1] "mizerExtensionTemplate" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "mizerExtensionTemplate" getBiomass(example_params)  # Plankton entry is present #>        Sprat      Herring          Cod     Plankton  #> 1.630305e+08 9.125316e+07 1.494402e+08 2.673020e+12 sim <- project(example_params, t_max = 5) plotBiomass(sim)

title: Using and adapting the mizer extension template
code: params <- newExtensionTemplateParams(NS_species_params) sim    <- project(params, t_max = 10) plotBiomass(sim)   # Plankton column appears automatically #> Warning in plotDataFrame(plot_dat, params, xlab = "Year", ylab = y_label, : #> missing legend in params@linecolour, some groups won't be displayed #> Warning: Removed 11 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`).

title: Using and adapting the mizer extension template
text: seasonal multiplier peaks t = 0.25 (quarter-year) troughs t = 0.75. default amplitude 0.2 encounter rate varies ±20 % around annual mean. seasonal effect implemented projectEncounter.mizerExtensionTemplate() (rate-methods.R). calls NextMethod() get base encounter rate (including plankton contribution), multiplies seasonal factor. Using project* method rather setRateFunction() means another extension package can also modify encounter rate without conflict.
code: params_s <- newExtensionTemplateParams(NS_species_params, season_amplitude = 0.4) enc_t0   <- getEncounter(params_s, t = 0)    # multiplier = 1.0 enc_t025 <- getEncounter(params_s, t = 0.25) # multiplier = 1.4 range(enc_t025 / enc_t0, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 1.4 1.4

title: Using and adapting the mizer extension template
text: plankton vector spectrum stored full resource size grid. time step: planktonEncounter() adds plankton encounter contribution getEncounter() presenting plankton extra resource. planktonDynamics() updates plankton solving ODE dP/dt = r(K − P) − m·P analytically time step, m predation mortality plankton spectrum.

what: Adapting this template for your extension
title: Using and adapting the mizer extension template
text: Rename package: search replace mizerExtensionTemplate → package name throughout files, including DESCRIPTION, R files, NAMESPACE, vignette. Decide: metadata-dispatching? Metadata-(like mizerStarvation): delete mizerExtensionTemplate-class.R, remove setClass() calls, remove coerceToExtensionClass() call end constructor. Keep params@extensions <- getRegisteredExtensions(). Dispatching (like mizerShelf): keep everything define S3 methods generics need override. Remove mechanisms don’t need: five mechanisms constructor.R independent. Delete blocks apply extension. Replace plankton component component, remove setComponent() entirely don’t need dynamical state variable. Run devtools::document() regenerate NAMESPACE roxygen2 tags, devtools::test() devtools::check() publishing.

title: Using and adapting the mizer extension template
text: setClass("<myExt>", contains = "MizerParams") setClass("<myExt>Sim", contains = "MizerSim") class file. mizer::registerExtension(pkgname, requirement = ...) .onLoad. Constructor ends params@extensions <- getRegisteredExtensions() coerceToExtensionClass(params). every MizerParams MizerSim object bundled data/, add makeActiveBinding call .onLoad (see mizerExtensionTemplate-package.R). Every S3 method registered via @method + @export. Every S3 method calls NextMethod(). Rate modifications use project* methods, setRateFunction(). Extension-specific state lives other_params(params) component params — new S4 slots.

title: Authors and Citation
text: First Last. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Last F (2026). mizerExtensionTemplate: Template mizer Extension Packages. R package version 0.0.0.9001, https://sizespectrum.org/mizerExtensionTemplate.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mizerExtensionTemplate: Template for mizer Extension Packages},   author = {First Last},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9001},   url = {https://sizespectrum.org/mizerExtensionTemplate}, }

title: Template for mizer Extension Packages
text: template R package building mizer extension packages. Clone , rename , replace placeholder code extension logic. See Get Started details.

title: Template for mizer Extension Packages
text: Every extension mechanism described mizer documentation illustrated working, commented code: template also shows kinds extension package: Dispatching (like template mizerShelf): defines marker S4 class mizer’s generic functions dispatch extension-specific methods via NextMethod(). Metadata-(like mizerStarvation): records extension dependency without overriding generic. Comments constructor explain parts keep drop case.

title: Template for mizer Extension Packages
code: # Install from GitHub (requires the development version of mizer) pak::pak("sizespectrum/mizerExtensionTemplate")

title: Template for mizer Extension Packages
code: library(mizerExtensionTemplate)  # Use the bundled example model (already correctly classed): getBiomass(example_params)   # includes Plankton  # Or build your own: params <- newExtensionTemplateParams(NS_species_params) sim    <- project(params, t_max = 10) plotBiomass(sim)   # includes a Plankton column

what: Using this template for your own extension
title: Template for mizer Extension Packages
text: Use repo GitHub template (click “Use template” GitHub) copy files manually. Rename mizerExtensionTemplate → package name throughout: DESCRIPTION (Package, Title, Description, URL, BugReports) R/mizerExtensionTemplate-class.R — rename file setClass calls R/mizerExtensionTemplate-package.R — update requirement string R/constructor.R — rename newExtensionTemplateParams() R/rate-methods.R R/generic-methods.R — rename method suffixes tests/ vignettes/ — rename file names internal references Decide: metadata-dispatching? metadata-extension, delete R/mizerExtensionTemplate-class.R, remove setClass() calls, drop coerceToExtensionClass() call end constructor. Keep params@extensions <- getRegisteredExtensions(). Replace placeholder extension logic . mechanism R/constructor.R independent — delete ones don’t need. ship bundled data object data/, add makeActiveBinding call .onLoad (see R/mizerExtensionTemplate-package.R) users always get correctly classed object regardless load order. Regenerate namespace documentation:

title: Template for mizer Extension Packages
text: Extending mizer — five extension mechanisms worked examples. Creating mizer extension package — concepts behind marker classes, NextMethod() chaining, composable extensions. Using mizer extension packages — user’s perspective extension chain.

what: Example MizerParams object for the extension template — example_params
title: Example MizerParams object for the extension template — example_params
text: small three-species mizerExtensionTemplate model built subset North Sea species parameters bundled mizer (Sprat, Herring, Cod). demonstrates five extension mechanisms provided template package: extra encounter term, seasonal encounter multiplier, allometric background mortality, dynamical plankton component, overridden getBiomass() generic.

Objects class mizerExtensionTemplate created newExtensionTemplateParams(). Objects class mizerExtensionTemplateSim returned automatically project() called mizerExtensionTemplate params object.

what: Create a MizerParams object using the extension template — newExtensionTemplateParams
title: Create a MizerParams object using the extension template — newExtensionTemplateParams
text: constructor demonstrates five extension mechanisms available mizer. Read inline comments understand block adapt extension.

title: Create a MizerParams object using the extension template — newExtensionTemplateParams
code: newExtensionTemplateParams(   species_params,   season_amplitude = 0.2,   extra_food_coef = 0.1,   background_mort_coef = 0.05,   plankton_rate = 0.5,   ... )

title: Create a MizerParams object using the extension template — newExtensionTemplateParams
text: species_params data frame species parameters passed directly mizer::newMultispeciesParams(). season_amplitude Amplitude sinusoidal seasonal variation encounter rate, fraction base rate. 0 disables effect; default 0.2 gives ±20 % variation year. extra_food_coef Coefficient allometric extra food source added via setExtEncounter(). Set 0 disable. background_mort_coef Coefficient background mortality added via setExtMort(). Set 0 disable. plankton_rate Intrinsic growth rate plankton component (yr⁻¹). Higher values make plankton respond faster depletion. ... Additional arguments passed mizer::newMultispeciesParams().

title: Create a MizerParams object using the extension template — newExtensionTemplateParams
text: MizerParams object class "mizerExtensionTemplate".

title: Create a MizerParams object using the extension template — newExtensionTemplateParams
text: setExtEncounter() / setExtMort() — add fixed, species × size arrays encounter mortality without dynamics. project* S3 method — seasonal encounter multiplier implemented projectEncounter.mizerExtensionTemplate(), replacing setRateFunction() approach. setComponent() — dynamical plankton component time-evolution encounter contribution. Defined component-functions.R. S3 generic overrides — getBiomass.mizerExtensionTemplate() getBiomass.mizerExtensionTemplateSim() add plankton biomass standard output.

title: Create a MizerParams object using the extension template — newExtensionTemplateParams
text: constructor creates dispatching extension: returned object class "mizerExtensionTemplate" mizer's generic functions dispatch S3 methods defined package. metadata-extension (one override generic), omit setClass() definition mizerExtensionTemplate-class.R coerceToExtensionClass() call end function. still call params@extensions <- getRegisteredExtensions() dependency recorded.

title: Plankton dynamics — planktonDynamics
text: Updates plankton component spectrum time step. function registered dynamics_fun "plankton" component newExtensionTemplateParams() called automatically project().

title: Plankton dynamics — planktonDynamics
Plankton dynamics — planktonDynamics
text: params MizerParams object. n_other Named list current component states. plankton spectrum available n_other[["plankton"]]. rates named list rates returned mizer::getRates(). Used compute predation mortality plankton spectrum via rates$pred_rate. dt Length current time step (years). component Name component ("plankton"). ... Additional arguments (ignored).

title: Plankton dynamics — planktonDynamics
text: Updated plankton abundance vector (full size grid).

title: Plankton dynamics — planktonDynamics
text: plankton grows towards size-specific carrying capacity fixed rate simultaneously depleted predation. update analytical solution linear ODE: $$dP/dt = r(K - P) - m \cdot P = rK - (r + m)P$$ r growth rate, K carrying capacity, m predation mortality plankton spectrum size bin. implementation follows detritus example vignette("extending-mizer", package = "mizer").

title: Plankton encounter contribution — planktonEncounter
text: Computes extra encounter rate fish experience feeding plankton component. function registered encounter_fun "plankton" component newExtensionTemplateParams() called automatically mizer::getEncounter() time step.

title: Plankton encounter contribution — planktonEncounter
Plankton encounter contribution — planktonEncounter
text: params MizerParams object. n Species abundance matrix (species × size). n_pp Resource abundance vector (full size grid). n_other Named list component states, including current plankton spectrum n_other[["plankton"]]. component Name component ("plankton"). ... Additional arguments (ignored).

title: Plankton encounter contribution — planktonEncounter
text: numeric matrix (species × size) extra encounter rates due plankton.

title: Plankton encounter contribution — planktonEncounter
text: implementation taken directly detritus example vignette("extending-mizer", package = "mizer"). treats plankton spectrum resource (n_pp) standard mizer encounter calculation, first removing plankton's encounter hook temporary params copy avoid double-counting.

what: Seasonal encounter rate for the extension template — projectEncounter.mizerExtensionTemplate

text: Applies sinusoidal seasonal multiplier top encounter rate computed lower extensions base mizer calculation.

code: # S3 method for class 'mizerExtensionTemplate' projectEncounter(params, n, n_pp, n_other, t = 0, ...)

title: Seasonal encounter rate for the extension template — projectEncounter.mizerExtensionTemplate
text: params MizerParams object class "mizerExtensionTemplate". n Species abundance matrix (species × size). n_pp Resource abundance vector (full size grid). n_other Named list component states. t Current simulation time (years). seasonal multiplier 1 + amplitude * sin(2π t), peaks t = 0.25 troughs t = 0.75. ... Additional arguments passed dispatch chain.

title: Seasonal encounter rate for the extension template — projectEncounter.mizerExtensionTemplate
text: numeric matrix (species × size) encounter rates, seasonally scaled.

title: Seasonal encounter rate for the extension template — projectEncounter.mizerExtensionTemplate
text: method replaces setRateFunction() approach used single-user scripts. participates S3 dispatch chain multiple loaded extension packages can modify encounter rate without overwriting one another.

what: Why project* methods instead of setRateFunction()
title: Seasonal encounter rate for the extension template — projectEncounter.mizerExtensionTemplate
text: setRateFunction(params, "Encounter", "myFn") stores single function name per rate. two extension packages call rate, second silently overwrites first. S3 dispatch via project* methods avoids : extension defines method marker class calls NextMethod() pass control chain.

title: Seasonal encounter rate for the extension template — projectEncounter.mizerExtensionTemplate
text: Always call NextMethod() — omitting silently drops contributions lower extensions base mizer calculation. Keep argument signature generic include ... extra arguments pass lower methods. call setRateFunction() constructor rate handled project* method package.
